steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side of the blank becomes an independent clause once the blank supplies “groups”: “The 20th century saw the founding of many Latino advocacy groups.” The material after the period begins with the participial phrase “Established to protect and advance the rights of Latino Americans,” followed by an independent clause about the American GI Forum and the Mexican American Legal Defense and Educational Fund.

steps.title: Apply the comma splice rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses cannot be joined by a comma alone. The correct answer uses a period to properly separate the two independent clauses and begins the second sentence with the participial phrase “Established to protect and advance,” which correctly modifies the subject of the following clause.

Joining two independent clauses with only a comma or no punctuation violates the comma splice rule; the phrase “groups and established” is also ungrammatical.

That is the correct answer.
